message:
feat(scanner): add heartbeat, cancellation, and backpressure to directory walker

Pass a heartbeat callback into process_directory and invoke it every
256 entries so the daemon stays alive while iterating large flat
directories.

Check the cancellation flag inside the per-entry hot loop so scans
can stop promptly when a budget expires, rather than waiting for an
entire directory read to finish.

Replace the unbounded result_tx.send() with send_timeout() in a
cancel-aware retry loop, preventing the walker from blocking
indefinitely when the result channel is full.

Reformat the work_tx.send_timeout() call for readability.

Add a regression test that verifies process_directory exits promptly
when the result channel is backpressured and cancellation is requested.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
